candidates.niche_name: Routing-Aware Optimizers for Sparse Expert-Choice Attention
candidates.mechanism: Expert-choice routing dynamically routes tokens to each attention head (from A), and this induced sparse, head-specialized attention structure is used to precondition the optimizer's per-parameter updates so that it produces faster, more stable convergence (à la B's accelerated optimization).
candidates.transfer: The expert-choice token-routing mechanism from A transfers to B as a structural prior that an optimizer exploits to accelerate convergence.
candidates.open_problem: Can MoSA-style expert-choice attention routing be coupled with a Conda-style optimizer to causally produce 2-2.5x faster convergence on LLaMA-scale models?
candidates.primary_quote: Conda achieves 2-2.5x faster convergence than AdamW on LLaMA series

candidates.niche_name: Preconditioned Memory Synthesis for Faster Test-Time Scaling
candidates.mechanism: Conda's geometry-aware preconditioning, which induces 2-2.5x faster convergence by routing gradient updates along better-conditioned directions, is transferred into MaTTS so that conditioned update steps accelerate the synthesis of higher-quality memory from scaled agent interactions.
candidates.transfer: The fast-convergence preconditioning mechanism from A transfers to B as the optimizer that compresses noisy scaled experiences into stable memory faster.
candidates.open_problem: Does applying Conda-style preconditioning to memory synthesis in MaTTS reduce the number of agent interactions needed to reach a target memory quality?
candidates.primary_quote: scales up agent interactions to generate diverse experiences that provide signals for synthesizing higher-quality memory

candidates.niche_name: Expert-Choice Routing of Agent Experiences for Memory Synthesis
candidates.mechanism: Expert-choice routing dynamically selects which agent interactions to attend to per memory-synthesis head, routing diverse test-time experiences into sparse high-signal subsets that produce higher-quality synthesized memory, which in turn regulates more effective scaling.
candidates.transfer: MoSA's expert-choice routing for dynamically selecting tokens transfers to selecting which scaled agent experiences feed memory synthesis.
candidates.open_problem: Can expert-choice routing over agent experiences select the sparse subset that maximizes synthesized memory quality under a fixed test-time interaction budget?
candidates.primary_quote: expert choice routing that dynamically selects tokens for each attention head
